evidence: Double yellow solid center lines with white outer edge lines are standard road markings in both Brazil and Colombia.
Reddish/orange soil is a prominent feature of the Brazilian Cerrado region (Central-West) and parts of the Colombian Llanos.
The savanna-like landscape with flat grassy fields and scattered deciduous trees matches the Cerrado biome in Brazil (e.g., Mato Grosso or Goiás).
A group of palm trees in a savanna context is characteristic of the 'veredas' in Brazil or the flooded savannas of Colombia.
White perimeter walls with decorative pillars are common architectural elements for rural estate (fazenda) entrances in Brazil.
contradictions: The presence of double yellow lines and reddish soil makes Colombia a strong alternative, as the Llanos Orientales shares many landscape similarities with the Brazilian Cerrado.
